question_results.question: List the names of schools with more than 30 difference in enrollements between K-12 and ages 5-17? Please also give the full street adress of the schools.
question_results.gold_sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Street FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` > 30
question_results.predicted_sql:
SELECT f."School Name", s.Street, s.City, s.State, s.Zip
FROM frpm AS f
JOIN schools AS s ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode
WHERE (f."Enrollment (K-12)" - f."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)") > 30
question_results.normalized_sql:
SELECT
  f."School Name",
  s.Street,
  s.City,
  s.State,
  s.Zip
FROM frpm AS f
JOIN schools AS s
  ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode
WHERE
  (
    f."Enrollment (K-12)" - f."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)"
  ) > 30

question_results.question: Please list the codes of the schools with a total enrollment of over 500.
question_results.gold_sql: SELECT T2.CDSCode FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` + T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` > 500
question_results.predicted_sql:
SELECT T1."School Code"
FROM frpm AS T1
WHERE (T1."Enrollment (K-12)" + T1."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)") > 500
question_results.normalized_sql:
SELECT
  T1."School Code"
FROM frpm AS T1
WHERE
  (
    T1."Enrollment (K-12)" + T1."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)"
  ) > 500

question_results.question: How many schools with an average score in Math greater than 400 in the SAT test are exclusively virtual?
question_results.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.School) FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.Virtual = 'F' AND T1.AvgScrMath > 400
question_results.predicted_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM satscores AS s
JOIN schools AS sch ON s.cds = sch.CDSCode
WHERE s.AvgScrMath > 400 AND sch.Virtual = 'F'
question_results.normalized_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*)
FROM satscores AS s
JOIN schools AS sch
  ON s.cds = sch.CDSCode
WHERE
  s.AvgScrMath > 400 AND sch.Virtual = 'F'

question_results.question: Among the schools with an SAT excellence rate of over 0.3, what is the highest eligible free rate for students aged 5-17?
question_results.gold_sql: SELECT MAX(CAST(T1.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) / T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE CAST(T2.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / T2.NumTstTakr > 0.3
question_results.predicted_sql:
SELECT MAX(CAST(f."Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)" AS REAL) / f."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)") AS max_eligible_free_rate
FROM (
    SELECT s.cds, s.NumGE1500, s.NumTstTakr, f."Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)", f."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)"
    FROM satscores AS s
    JOIN frpm AS f ON s.cds = f.CDSCode
    WHERE CAST(s.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / s.NumTstTakr > 0.3
) AS f
question_results.normalized_sql:
SELECT
  MAX(CAST(f."Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)" AS REAL) / f."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)") AS max_eligible_free_rate
FROM (
  SELECT
    s.cds,
    s.NumGE1500,
    s.NumTstTakr,
    f."Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)",
    f."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)"
  FROM satscores AS s
  JOIN frpm AS f
    ON s.cds = f.CDSCode
  WHERE
    CAST(s.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / s.NumTstTakr > 0.3
) AS f

question_results.question: Rank schools by their average score in Writing where the score is greater than 499, showing their charter numbers.
question_results.gold_sql: SELECT CharterNum, AvgScrWrite, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AvgScrWrite DESC) AS WritingScoreRank FROM schools AS T1  INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T2.AvgScrWrite > 499 AND CharterNum is not null
question_results.predicted_sql:
SELECT T2.School, T2.CharterNum 
FROM satscores AS T1 
INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode 
WHERE T1.AvgScrWrite > 499 AND T2.CharterNum IS NOT NULL 
ORDER BY T1.AvgScrWrite DESC
question_results.normalized_sql:
SELECT
  T2.School,
  T2.CharterNum
FROM satscores AS T1
INNER JOIN schools AS T2
  ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode
WHERE
  T1.AvgScrWrite > 499 AND NOT T2.CharterNum IS NULL
ORDER BY
  T1.AvgScrWrite DESC
